subject: feat: decompose prototype into data-driven content architecture
decision: roster is knight/ranger/wizard via palette swaps; rogue retained as data.
why: prototype counted only the 2 entities named 'Forest Orc' toward a 4-kill quota — unwinnable.

subject: feat: decompose prototype into data-driven content architecture
decision: quest kill-counter matches all forest archetypes.
why: prototype referenced undefined palette keys and an undefined drawPixelSprite — it does not run as exported.

subject: feat: decompose prototype into data-driven content architecture
decision: added Y/O colors to base palette; castle is a map-placed prop.

subject: feat: scaffold Vite + React + Vitest toolchain with GPU headed browser tests
decision: vitest browser provider is @vitest/browser-playwright (Vitest 4 API).
why: string provider form is the deprecated v3 shape; v4 d.ts documents the factory import.

subject: feat: adopt r3f HD-2D renderer — research validated, pixi dropped
decision: three.js via @react-three/fiber sole world renderer; DOM UI.
why: spike screenshots judged r3f 2.5D higher calibre than the pixi 2D baseline; three ships RenderPixelatedPass + frustum snapping first-party; pixi lighting plugin is v7-dead; one renderer beats hybrid complexity.

subject: feat: yuka enemy AI — steering-driven behaviors per archetype
decision: flee panicDistance set from archetype keepDistance.
why: yuka FleeBehavior defaults to a 10px panic radius and produces zero force beyond it — kiting silently never moved.

subject: feat: app runtime, mobile HUD, and button-driven playthrough
decision: default gameplay HUD is one-line top chrome; quest log, minimap, and audio state live in a right slideout.
why: mobile play area must stay at or above 80% world view; large persistent HUD panels reduce phone usability.

subject: feat: app runtime, mobile HUD, and button-driven playthrough
decision: bridge crossings require at least three repaired rows and dungeon door apertures require five tiles in this map scale.
why: headed button-driven playthrough proved one-tile apertures clip the configured player hitbox and are not reliably traversable.

subject: feat: S5.5 menus without CRT filter
decision: CRT and scanline effects are excluded from the UI/menu slice.
why: The target is FF7-era/HD-2D diorama staging; emulator filters would cheapen the presentation and do not serve mobile gameplay.

subject: feat: Old Road Diorama design language
decision: Typography, UI colors, panel grammar, menu motion, and r3f material treatment now come from an owned Old Road Diorama language before component styling.
why: The previous UI read as generic 16-bit arcade chrome; the game needs its own FF7/HD-2D fantasy interface language with shadered depth and tokenized roles.

subject: feat: Errant Storybook landing and local typography
decision: Errant Storybook is the active design language: local IM Fell English SC display, EB Garamond body, Alegreya Sans HUD, parchment/vellum UI, morning-gold landing page, and no cyberpunk glass or CRT effects.
why: The previous visual language still read too cyberpunk/arcade and did not match the requested knights-errant storybook tone.

subject: feat: Drizzle and Capacitor SQLite save shell
decision: Landing shell owns New Game, Continue, and Settings; save slots use Drizzle schema plus Capacitor SQLite, Preferences stores small settings, and web SQLite pins sql.js@1.11.0 for jeep-sqlite@2.8.0.
why: Live headed browser validation caught sql.js@1.14.x wasm failing inside jeep-sqlite; the real save path must be validated by auto-save, reload, Continue enabled, and Continue back into gameplay.

subject: chore: dependabot respects pinned save stack
decision: Dependabot ignores @capacitor-community/sqlite, @capacitor/core, @capacitor/preferences, jeep-sqlite, and sql.js; these dependencies move only as a manually validated compatibility set.
why: Piecemeal dependency updates can recreate the jeep-sqlite/sql.js wasm import mismatch that broke the live web save path.
overrides: generic dependabot npm updates for pinned persistence packages

subject: feat: S6 world plan and village interiors
decision: Portal triggers use content-declared toMap/toSpawn and destination maps expose named spawns; map loads carry spawnId instead of hard-coding door behavior in app code.
why: Village house/shop/tavern interiors need reversible door transitions while preserving the content-first architecture and button-driven browser validation.

subject: fix: portal spawn geometry
decision: Return spawns must sit outside outbound exterior door zones, and interior entry spawns must sit outside their own return-door zones.
why: The headed browser interior test proved a spawn inside the return trigger causes an immediate auto-return before the player can observe or control the interior.

subject: feat: S6.3 exterior route and shop interaction depth
decision: Exterior route depth now requires authored landmarks and browser-walkable portals across Hearthwake Village, Oldwood Forest, Deep Forest, and Castle Approach; the player governor validates the route through public directional input.
why: Longer maps that are only big cross pathways still feel shallow; route content must include tiles, props, NPC placements, and screenshot evidence.

subject: feat: S6.3 shopkeeper interaction
decision: Hearthwake Shop has a shopkeeper NPC with stateful dialogue and a one-time travel-cake heal implemented through quest effects.
why: A shop interior with only boxes/chests is not a real interaction; the first shop verb must mutate game state through the content-first story pipeline.

subject: feat: S6.4 questline depth
decision: The expanded route now has seven authored quests, including Morning Errands, Oldwood Oath, and Lost Page Rowan, with Page Pip, Oldwood Hermit, and Lost Page Rowan dialogue banks and reachable map placements.
why: Depth requires midpoint story graphs and visible NPC interaction, not only larger maps. Unit tests validate quest reducers and browser tests validate public movement plus A-button dialogue touchpoints.

subject: test: split browser gate into core and journey invocations
decision: `pnpm test:browser` runs the same Vitest browser project in two headed Playwright invocations: core visual/persistence/UI specs, then long journey specs.
why: The one-shot aggregate browser run hung after all specs were added, while the grouped invocations passed reliably and still preserve GPU browser validation coverage.
overrides: single aggregate browser invocation for all specs

subject: feat: S6.5 regional enemy depth
decision: Enemy depth is now region-scoped through enemies.difficultyCurve plus new Oldwood, Deep Forest, and Castle Approach archetypes placed on the expanded route maps.
why: The world needs regions that play differently, not only higher HP values. Config owns threat order, archetype membership, names, palettes, hitboxes, ranges, cooldowns, and map placements.

subject: feat: Yuka ambush and guard interpreters
decision: `ambush` and `guard` are generic Yuka behavior interpreters in enemyAIStep; bramble stalkers hold until trigger, while gate enemies chase inside a leash and return to their post.
why: Behavior variety belongs in reusable AI modes, with JSON tuning per archetype, so future maps can add regional enemies without hard-coded map logic.

subject: feat: S6.6 expanded playthrough
decision: New Game starts in Hearthwake Village and the browser journey now proves Village -> Oldwood -> Sunken Road -> Castle Approach -> Dungeon -> Victory with real keyboard controls.
why: The vertical-slice overworld route no longer represented the authored game. The main route must exercise the expanded maps, quest gates, key fight, save/Continue path, and final rescue.
overrides: original two-map-only playthrough as the end-to-end proof

subject: test: split expanded journey through real Continue
decision: The long playthrough is split into road-to-dungeon and Continue-to-victory browser specs joined by the app's real MemorySaveRepository autosave.
why: A single long headed browser spec could terminate before diagnostics flushed. Splitting at the real Continue boundary preserves public-control validation and keeps Vitest browser stable.

subject: fix: Sunken Road visual staging
decision: Large ruin silhouettes sit upstage from the key-fight lane, and the Sandwyrm route uses Sunken Road props plus a readable old-stone crossing instead of a black foreground slab.
why: Screenshot review showed the first Sunken Road staging read as a giant black slab in the 2.5D camera; visual tests must catch composition failures, not just nonblank canvases.

subject: feat: S7.1 Capacitor Android scaffold
decision: The Android platform is committed as a first-class product target with capacitor.config.ts, native Gradle scaffold, @capacitor/android, @capacitor/device, @capacitor/cli, and explicit cap:sync/cap:run scripts.
why: Shipping Android cannot rely on local generation or untracked native state; native plugin metadata, Gradle files, manifest, and web asset sync need repeatable tests and commands.
overrides: browser-only shell as the shipping target

subject: feat: Capacitor device profile HUD
decision: The app shell resolves a device profile from @capacitor/device plus viewport metrics and exposes data-device-profile for CSS and browser tests; phone profiles hide wide HP/XP bars and use safe-area-aware chrome.
why: Mobile phones need at least 80% gameplay area, while tablets/foldables can keep richer readouts. Breakpoints alone cannot represent native platform and form-factor state.
overrides: CSS breakpoint only mobile HUD decisions

subject: test: Android native gate
decision: S7.1 validation includes pnpm cap:sync and android ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug, in addition to lint/typecheck/unit/browser-core/browser-journey.
why: Capacitor sync proves web assets and plugin metadata copy, but the debug APK assemble proves the generated Android project is actually buildable.
overrides: cap sync as the only native validation

subject: feat: S7.1 Capacitor Android scaffold
decision: Android is a committed Capacitor platform target with app id com.jbogaty.goodoldfashionedadventure, webDir dist, cap:sync script, native SQLite/Device/Preferences plugins, and a passing debug assemble.
why: Shipping to Android is part of the game surface. `cap sync` proves web/native asset sync, while `./gradlew :app:assembleDebug` catches native resource and plugin wiring failures before CI.

subject: feat: Capacitor device profile for mobile HUD density
decision: The app shell exposes `data-device-profile` from `@capacitor/device` plus viewport measurements; phones hide HP/XP bars and keep controls as safe-area-aware overlays, while tablets/desktops can show bars.
why: The mobile 80% gameplay-area rule needs platform-aware density, not only CSS breakpoints. Browser tests assert the live DOM profile, unit tests classify phone/tablet/desktop profiles, and screenshots are read for the compact HUD.

subject: feat: S7.2 CI and release pipeline
decision: CI runs lint, typecheck, unit tests, the split Vitest browser command with CI headless GPU flags, production build, cap sync, Android debug assemble, and uploads the debug APK artifact.
why: The repo needs the same gates locally and remotely. Browser playthrough coverage and Android packaging are product requirements, not optional release tasks.

subject: chore: release-please manifest mode and standard repo docs
decision: release-please owns CHANGELOG/package version state through release-please-config.json plus .release-please-manifest.json, and root standard docs now describe testing, deployment, state, and repo standards.
why: S7.3 needs a branch that can produce reviewable CI and releases without stale pre-scaffold docs or implicit release state.

subject: fix: headed GPU browser CI and serialized browser specs
decision: The CI browser job runs on macOS with VITEST_BROWSER_HEADLESS=false, while the Vitest browser project sets fileParallelism=false.
why: GitHub Ubuntu reported ANGLE SwiftShader despite GPU flags, failing the real-GPU smoke test. Parallel browser spec files also shared the public page and let independent specs collide under CI load.

subject: fix: local Tone BGM scheduling
decision: BGM timing is owned by each createToneAudioEngine instance with a window timer; the engine must not use Tone.Transport or Tone.Loop for app-mounted playback.
why: Hosted headed browser CI passed the gameplay specs but failed on an unhandled Tone start-time error from global transport state leaking across app mounts. Instance-local timing keeps ToneJS sound without cross-test scheduler state.

subject: fix: deterministic governor point steering
decision: Close-range browser specs use PlayerGovernor.reachPoint for keyboard-driven coordinate steering, then assert player-visible outcomes such as dialogue text.
why: The shop map deliberately places Keeper Brindle and Mage Gwydion close together. A fixed-duration upward hold could end closer to the mage under CI timing, while shared point steering keeps the playthrough public-input-only and deterministic.

subject: fix: CLI-level browser serialization
decision: The browser package scripts pass --browser.fileParallelism=false and --no-file-parallelism in addition to the Vitest project config.
why: MacOS headed CI still interleaved browser spec files and let the shop test observe dialogue from another mounted app. Serialization must live on the exact command CI runs, not only in config.

subject: fix: pause clears movement input synchronously
decision: Pause/resume paths clear directional input immediately, and direction keydowns while paused do not set movement intent.
why: Hosted headed CI caught a 2.4px movement drift while paused. The pause command must stop player movement before the next RAF tick, not only after React commits paused state.

subject: fix: address PR review blockers without admin bypass
decision: Actionable Amazon Q and Gemini review threads are product fixes: production logging off, Android backup off, branch trigger cleaned, leap collision checked, ground texture disposed, SQLite HMR reconnect guarded, autosave interval config-backed at 15s, and Tone sequence notes use pooled synth voices.
why: The main ruleset requires review-thread resolution. Admin bypass would merge around concrete security/runtime issues instead of hardening the game and its ship path.

subject: feat: S8.1 castle-interior route depth
decision: The key-gated Castle Approach portal now leads through Castle Yard, Castle Hall, Castle Library, and Castle Armory before the final dungeon; the route is governed by quest:castle-letters and scribe dialogue.
why: A direct road-to-dungeon teleport made the authored journey too shallow. Depth requires interior room verbs, NPC interaction, and public-control browser proof through each room.
overrides: Castle Approach direct portal to map:castle-dungeon

subject: fix: persist map loads immediately
decision: Map-load snapshots persist immediately in addition to the configured autosave interval.
why: The split headed playthrough reached the dungeon but Continue still saw the previous hall save when the interval had not fired. Save boundaries must reflect the current room after route transitions.

subject: feat: extend terrain families and add sunken wash route verb
decision: repeated border and hazard surfaces (mountain, water,
why: they repeat in every route window read and were the last flat

subject: docs: reframe upgrade structure as a proper DAG
decision: DAG over spiderweb for the upgrade structure.
why: directed all-prerequisite gating is what the runtime already

subject: feat: realign economy to rose majors and coin rank tracks
decision: rank state is a separate upgradeRanks record, not repeated
why: prerequisites and save sanitization keep their set semantics and

subject: feat: boot new games onto the rescue route
decision: baseline guardian is a new dragon-guardian archetype, not
why: the first run must be winnable by a fresh knight per the

subject: feat: make purchased ranks shape the next run
decision: GameStage cleanup deliberately does not dispose uMap
why: textureFor caches one shared CanvasTexture per atlas canvas —

subject: fix: keep the trail timer through the projectile's life write
decision: assert ghost cadence via FxStats spawn counter over 0.5s
why: existence-only assertions passed while the timer reset every frame

subject: feat: choreograph the bosses into readable phases
decision: phase state lives in a Choreo trait, not the AI WeakMap
why: the renderer and playthrough tests must read the same public state

subject: fix: stop browser test lists rotting out of CI
decision: directory sweep + excluded-equals-journey invariant test
why: hand-maintained include lists already rotted once unnoticed

subject: fix: read the boss phase without per-frame allocations
feat: make warband ranks reinforce the field
decision: bounty rides the enemy:defeated event, not a spawner-side ledger
why: the quest reducer is the single reward chokepoint; events already carry archetypeId

subject: test: gate the warband trade against dead wiring and sharp edges
decision: spawnBounty is the opt-in marker for reinforcement consumption
why: enemyFamily alone overloads taxonomy majors with count-rank behavior

subject: test: prove the warband trade in the live journey
fix: fold S13 review findings forward
